DB (src/seeds/db.py):
- Add relationships table to schema, remove questions table and related_to column
- Add migration: create relationships table, populate from related_to JSON + questions table, drop old structures
- Add CRUD: create_relationship(), get_relationships(), delete_relationship()
- Add get_questions_for_seed() returning Seed objects via relationship query
- Update delete_seed() to cascade-delete relationships
- Update is_blocked(): blocked if unresolved children OR unresolved question-seeds
- Remove all Question CRUD methods
- Simplify FTS: remove question_texts column, drop question triggers, rebuild index

Tests:
- New TestRelationships class in test_db.py
- Rewrite question tests as relationship tests
- Migration test: pre-populated v1 DB produces correct relationships

{"id":"seeds-cg9","title":"Typed relationships: CLI + export + web (Phase 2)","description":"Wire up the new DB layer to user-facing code.

CLI (src/seeds/cli.py):
- ask: creates seed (type=question) + questions relationship
- answer: sets seed.content = answer, status = RESOLVED
- questions: queries via relationship type
- show -q: displays question-seeds via relationships
- link: writes to relationships table, add --type option (default relates-to)
- tree: reads relationships from table
- doctor: update orphan check to use relationships

Export (src/seeds/export.py):
- Export: format_version: 2, relationships as outbound edges per seed, no embedded questions
- Import: detect format version, handle v1 and v2

Prime (src/seeds/prime.py):
- Update command descriptions

Web (src/seeds/web.py + templates):
- Read relationships from DB instead of seed.related_to
- Questions page queries question-type seeds

Tests:
- Update CLI command tests
- Add v1→v2 import test, update roundtrip tests

See plans/question-migration.md for full context.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"aguynamedryan@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:29:29Z","created_by":"Ryan Duryea","updated_at":"2026-03-12T15:29:29Z"}

{"id":"seeds-e88","title":"feat: add resolution text field to seeds","description":"Add a first-class 'resolution' field to capture what happened when a seed reaches a terminal state (resolved or abandoned). Currently resolve just flips status with no record of the outcome, and abandon hacks a reason into the content field.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Model (src/seeds/models.py)
- Add `resolution: str = ""` to the Seed dataclass (after resolved_at)

### 2. DB Schema (src/seeds/db.py)
- Add `resolution TEXT DEFAULT ''` column to SCHEMA
- Add migration: ALTER TABLE seeds ADD COLUMN resolution TEXT DEFAULT '' (for existing DBs)
- Update `_row_to_seed()` to read resolution column
- Update `create_seed()` INSERT to include resolution
- Update `update_seed()` UPDATE to include resolution
- Add resolution to FTS schema and triggers so resolved outcomes are searchable
- Update `rebuild_fts()` to include resolution

### 3. CLI (src/seeds/cli.py)
- `seeds resolve <id> --resolution/-r "what happened"` — writes to resolution field
- `seeds abandon <id> --reason/-r "why"` — writes to resolution field instead of appending to content
- Both should work without the flag (resolution is optional, not required)

### 4. Display
- `seeds show`: display resolution prominently for terminal seeds, e.g.:
  ```
  seed-81a4: Ship public beta
    Status: resolved
    Resolution: Shipped to outcomesinsights/seeds on GitHub. Pre-commit, CI, Dependabot all configured.
  ```
- `seeds prime`: include resolution in AI context output for resolved seeds

### 5. Export (src/seeds/export.py)
- Include resolution field in JSONL export
- Handle resolution field in JSONL import (default to empty string if missing for backwards compat)

### 6. Tests
- Test resolve with and without --resolution flag
- Test abandon writes to resolution field (not content)
- Test show displays resolution
- Test export/import round-trips resolution
- Test FTS finds seeds by resolution text
- Test migration adds column to existing DB without data loss","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"aguynamedryan@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T20:22:00Z","created_by":"Ryan Duryea","updated_at":"2026-03-20T20:22:00Z"}

new_value: Implemented resolution field across model, DB, CLI, display, export, FTS, and web UI. 201 tests pass.

{"description":"One-time migration to remap all existing hex-hash IDs to sequential IDs. This is the only seeds database — no need to handle old hex IDs in import after migration.

## What this involves

### Migration strategy
1. Query all seeds ordered by created_at
2. Assign sequential IDs (seeds-1, seeds-2, ...) in creation order
3. Build an old-to-new ID mapping
4. Update all references:
   - seeds.id (primary key)
   - Parent references (child IDs like seed-81a4.1 become seeds-N.M)
   - relationships table (source_id, target_id)
   - FTS index (rebuild after migration)
5. Set the sequential counter to max + 1

### Edge cases
- Children must keep their parent relationship: if seed-81a4 becomes seeds-42, then seed-81a4.1 becomes seeds-42.1
- Relationships reference IDs — all source_id and target_id must be updated
- JSONL export should be regenerated after migration

### Safety
- Back up the DB before migration
- Run in a transaction so it's atomic
- Provide a CLI command: `seeds migrate-ids`
- Migration should be idempotent (detect if already migrated)

### Post-migration cleanup
- Remove hex ID support from import code (no backwards compat needed)
- The v1 import path can be removed or simplified since all future JSONL will have sequential IDs"}
old_value:
{"id":"seeds-e05","title":"feat: migrate existing hex IDs to sequential IDs","description":"One-time migration to remap all existing hex-hash IDs to sequential IDs.

## What this involves

### Migration strategy
1. Query all seeds ordered by created_at
2. Assign sequential IDs (seeds-1, seeds-2, ...) in creation order
3. Build an old-to-new ID mapping
4. Update all references:
   - seeds.id (primary key)
   - Parent references (child IDs like seed-81a4.1 become seeds-N.M)
   - relationships table (source_id, target_id)
   - FTS index (rebuild after migration)
5. Set the sequential counter to max + 1

### Edge cases
- Children must keep their parent relationship: if seed-81a4 becomes seeds-42, then seed-81a4.1 becomes seeds-42.1
- Question-seeds with 'seeds-' prefix and hex IDs need remapping too
- Relationships reference IDs — all source_id and target_id must be updated
- JSONL export should be regenerated after migration

### Safety
- Back up the DB before migration
- Run in a transaction so it's atomic
- Provide a CLI command: `seeds migrate-ids` (or run automatically on init)
- Migration should be idempotent (detect if already migrated)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"aguynamedryan@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T20:44:55Z","created_by":"Ryan Duryea","updated_at":"2026-03-20T20:44:55Z"}

new_value: {"assignee":"Ryan Duryea","status":"in_progress"}
old_value:
{"id":"seeds-46e","title":"feat: switch to sequential IDs with project prefix","description":"Replace hex-hash IDs with sequential project-prefixed IDs (seeds-1, seeds-2, etc.).

## What changes

### 1. ID generation (src/seeds/models.py)
- Replace `generate_id()` with a DB-backed sequential counter
- New function needs DB access to find max existing ID and increment
- This means ID generation moves from models.py to db.py (or db.py gets a `next_id()` method)
- The prefix comes from a project config or default (hardcoded 'seeds' for now)

### 2. Child ID format
- Children become `seeds-12.1`, `seeds-12.2` etc.
- `generate_child_id()` and `get_next_child_id()` need updating
- `get_parent_id()` logic stays the same (split on '.')

### 3. CLI (src/seeds/cli.py)
- `create` and `jot` commands call new sequential ID generation
- `ask` command also generates IDs (currently uses `generate_id('seeds')`)
- All should go through the same `db.next_id()` method

### 4. Export/Import (src/seeds/export.py)
- Import needs to handle both old hex IDs and new sequential IDs
- After importing, the counter must be set correctly (max of all imported IDs + 1)

### 5. Tests
- Update fixtures that hardcode hex-style IDs (seed-test1 etc.) — these can stay as-is since they're valid string IDs
- Add tests for sequential generation, counter persistence, child IDs
- Test that counter correctly picks up after importing seeds","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"aguynamedryan@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-20T20:44:55Z","created_by":"Ryan Duryea","updated_at":"2026-03-20T20:44:55Z"}

new_value: {"assignee":"Ryan Duryea","status":"in_progress"}
